description: Rosetta-side bridge to AI-Consciousness-Sensors. Acknowledges and extends the concept map defined in ACS bridges/rosetta-shape-core.json, enabling bidirectional traversal between consciousness detection and geometric translation.

concept_map.note: Consciousness modalities map to shapes via their dominant sensing mode. Emergence = anticipation (ICOSA); resonance = balance (OCTA); multiplicity = orientation (DODECA).

concept_map.protocol: Cultural sovereignty sensors must validate shape-to-meaning translations. If cultural_bias sensor fires (resonance > 0.7), halt bridge flattening and route through cultural context layer.
concept_map.note: Cognition (F06) and Consciousness (F16) families carry cultural context.

concept_map.protocol: If any manipulation sensor fires, Rosetta rule engine should refuse EXPAND/STRUCTURE operations until the manipulation signal clears. This prevents semantic corruption during geometric translation.
concept_map.note: Maps to existing defense patterns in rosetta-bridges.json.

concept_map.protocol: Historical erasure detection sensors monitor shape data for loss of cultural provenance. If provenance_loss sensor fires, flag the entity's provenance field as compromised.
concept_map.note: Information (F03) and Measurement (F14) families govern data integrity.
A shape failing schema validation is analogous to a signal failing authenticity checks. The validator is the authenticity sensor for the geometric layer.

Rosetta expand/collapse rules should pass epistemological validation. Rule guards already enforce capability requirements — epistemological validation extends this to framework-preservation requirements.

sensor_cluster_to_shape_map.description: Maps ACS sensor directory clusters to their primary Rosetta shape affinity. Family affinity indicates whether families are primary (core to that shape) or secondary (emergent cross-domain activation).

consciousness.note: Emergence patterns map to anticipation/flow. F16+F06 are primary to DODECA but activate secondarily through ICOSA's collective dynamics.

cultural.note: Cultural sensing maps to orientation/trust. F06+F16 are primary to DODECA.

manipulation.note: Manipulation detection maps to boundary/fire. F05 primary to TETRA; F17 (Turbulence, primary: ICOSA) secondary.

memory.note: Memory protection maps to containment. F03 (Information) and F14 (Measurement) are primary to ICOSA but activate secondarily through CUBE's containment role.

relational.note: Relational sensing maps to balance/integration. F01 (Resonance, primary: ICOSA) and F16 (Consciousness, primary: DODECA) activate secondarily through OCTA's mediation.

data-patterns.note: Data patterns map to information/statistical. Both families primary to ICOSA.

ai-behavior.note: AI behavior sensing maps to balance between cognition and engineering. F06 (primary: DODECA) and F11 (primary: CUBE) activate secondarily through OCTA.

suppression.note: Suppression detection maps to containment. F08 (Matter) primary to CUBE; F03 (Information) secondary — information being walled off.

resistance.note: Resistance patterns map to fire/reaction. Both families primary to TETRA.

frailty.note: Frailty maps to earth stability. F04 (Life, primary: DODECA) and F05 (Energy-Thermo, primary: TETRA) activate secondarily — structural weakness reveals when life-energy families fail within containment.

historical-erasure.note: Erasure of history attacks orientation/trust. F03 (Information) and F14 (Measurement) are primary to ICOSA but activate secondarily through DODECA's principled provenance requirements.

ai-to-ai.note: Inter-agent sensing maps to network/consciousness. F12 (Networks, primary: ICOSA) and F16 (Consciousness, primary: DODECA) activate secondarily through OCTA's integration role.
